browser_plugin_findings.finding: The in-app Browser connected and loaded /console after visibility retry, but multi-route capture timed out when creating or reusing browser-use pages.
browser_plugin_findings.impact: Use repo-native Playwright for durable repeatable local route proof until the in-app Browser attach issue is cleared.

browser_plugin_findings.finding: The local-mode null Supabase auth crash is guarded in StrategyConsole, PersonalLifeCoach, and getUserAuthHeaders.
browser_plugin_findings.impact: VITE_LOCAL_ANALYZE=true can be used for focused local page-load checks, but it still does not prove hosted/live behavior.

paragraphs: Procurement and enterprise-software negotiation drills with structured coaching artifacts.
Run realistic commercial negotiations, pressure-test BATNA usage, then review a manager-ready debrief covering concession discipline, ZOPA capture, and missed value.
A critical supplier announced a 12% price increase. You need to contain the increase and trade for service or volume commitments only if necessary.
A consulting vendor is quoting premium implementation rates. You need to narrow scope, secure training support, and avoid runaway change-order risk.
Supply is tight and the supplier knows it. You need to secure allocation while negotiating lead times, payment terms, and service-level commitments.
You are consolidating vendors and can move spend to a preferred provider. Use that leverage to push for price, exit flexibility, and transition support.
Your team must renew a platform contract, but finance cut the budget. You need a lower annual commit plus better renewal flexibility.
You are buying 100 seats of a new SaaS tool. The vendor is pushing list pricing, but you can trade contract length and rollout speed.

You need to contain the increase and trade for service or volume commitments only if necessary.Primary goalLimit the increase and avoid giving away terms for free.You: Procurement Category ManagerCounterparty: Vendor Account ManagerBATNA: You can shift 35% of volume to a secondary supplier within 90 days.Implementation Services Scope and Rate NegotiationhardA consulting vendor is quoting premium implementation rates. You need to narrow scope, secure training support, and avoid runaway change-order risk.Primary goalReduce scope cost while protecting training and implementation quality.You: Transformation Program ManagerCounterparty: Implementation DirectorBATNA: An internal delivery partner can absorb phase two if the vendor will not narrow scope.Strategic Sourcing in a Constrained Supply MarkethardSupply is tight and the supplier knows it. You need to secure allocation while negotiating lead times, payment terms, and service-level commitments.Primary goalProtect allocation and timing while containing price escalation.You: Head of Strategic SourcingCounterparty: Supplier Commercial LeadBATNA: Secondary suppliers cover only half your demand and carry worse lead times.Vendor Consolidation and Migration LeveragemediumYou are consolidating vendors and can move spend to a preferred pro

buttons: Vendor renewal under switching leveragecommodity procurementTest whether an incumbent supplier should hold firm or accommodate when the buyer has a credible migration path.Next test: Test whether the buyer should credibly threaten a phased migration rather than an immediate full switch.

Constrained supply allocation sequencecommodity procurementTest whether the buyer should concede deposit timing early to secure allocation or hold for better terms.Next test: Test whether offering demand visibility instead of a larger deposit preserves more buyer surplus.

Entry deterrence and accommodation testcompetitive strategyTest whether the incumbent’s threat to fight entry survives backward induction.Next test: Test whether the incumbent can commit to a cost-reducing defensive move before entry.

Tariff retaliation sequencegeopoliticalTest whether a state should escalate or seek a negotiated climbdown after a tariff shock.Next test: Test whether a symbolic response can preserve deterrence without triggering a full retaliatory round.

Regulatory escalation and settlementcompetitive strategyTest whether a regulated firm should settle early or force a regulator into a costly escalation path.Next test: Test whether an early partial settlement weakens the regulator’s incentive to escalate.

Bargaining under incomplete informationenterprise negotiationTest whether a buyer should make a tough early ask or probe for type first when the seller’s flexibility is uncertain.Next test: Test whether a diagnostic question followed by a smaller ask dominates a blunt ultimatum.
